runs.bet: Cost-intent readers care about the cash price plus the unpaid owner time. A stronger cost page should expose the hidden content-work cost and connect that pain to review-ready posts.
runs.expected_signal: Check impressions, clicks, CTR, average position, indexing status, and whether related buyer-intent pages receive stronger internal-link support over the next 2–6 weeks.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: A weak result may mean indexing delay, low demand, SERP competition, weak internal links, title mismatch, or execution quality. Do not turn one weak page into a durable rule without clearer evidence.

runs.bet: This page can earn more qualified SEO/AEO visibility if it answers the buyer's job more directly and routes the reader toward a preview-first next step.
runs.expected_signal: Check impressions, clicks, CTR, average position, indexing status, and whether related buyer-intent pages receive stronger internal-link support over the next 2–6 weeks.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: A weak result may mean indexing delay, low demand, SERP competition, weak internal links, title mismatch, or execution quality. Do not turn one weak page into a durable rule without clearer evidence.

runs.bet: Affordable social media management searches are not only price-comparison searches. The page is more index-worthy and conversion-ready when it explains that the cheapest option is only truly cheap if the content creation burden is handled.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the canonical page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether it gains impressions/clicks for affordable social media management, social media management under $100, low-cost social media management, and related small-business service queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume the page topic is weak. Possible causes include duplicate/competing affordable-management routes, insufficient internal links, crawl timing, the existing blog guide competing with the service page, or Google treating this as too similar to related pricing/cost pages.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/affordable-social-media-management

runs.bet: Comparison pages are more index-worthy when they answer the buyer's decision in the first screen and make the underlying workflow difference explicit. For Metricool vs Hootsuite, the important distinction is not only feature count or price; it is whether the business already has content ready to manage.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for Metricool vs Hootsuite, Metricool for small business, Hootsuite for small business, and scheduling-tool comparison queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume comparison pages are weak. Possible causes include crawl timing, SERP competition, duplicate/overlapping Hootsuite/Metricool comparison pages, not enough internal-link equity, or Google treating the page as too similar to other scheduler comparison pages.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/blog/metricool-vs-hootsuite-for-small-business

runs.bet: Alternative pages are more index-worthy when they name the exact buyer mismatch early. For Buffer alternatives, the mismatch is that Buffer helps publish posts the business already made, while many owners are really searching for a way to get posts created before scheduling.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for Buffer alternative with content creation, Buffer alternative small business, Buffer content creation, and scheduler-with-content queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume Buffer-alternative intent is weak. Possible causes include overlap with /compare/buffer-alternative, overlap with Buffer pricing/free-plan pages, crawl timing, insufficient internal-link equity, or Google treating this as too close to other scheduler comparison pages.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/blog/buffer-alternative-with-content-creation-built-in

runs.bet: Question pages are more index-worthy when they answer the direct buyer question immediately and connect the tool choice to the real workflow problem. For Google Business Profile posting, the key issue is not only which tool can publish to Google; it is who creates useful local updates every week.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for best Google Business Profile posting tool, GBP posting tool local business, and Google Business Profile posting service queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume GBP/AEO question pages are weak. Possible causes include overlap with the GBP service page and longer GBP tools guide, crawl timing, insufficient internal-link equity, or Google preferring the service/blog canonical over a short answer page.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/resources/questions/best-google-business-profile-posting-tool-local-business

runs.bet: GBP service-area pages are more index-worthy when they answer the setup question immediately and connect profile setup to ongoing local proof. Service-area businesses need to know what to show, what to hide, and how to keep the profile current enough to earn a call.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for Google Business Profile service area business, service-area business GBP, hide address GBP, and GBP posting for service area business queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume GBP/local SEO content is weak. Possible causes include overlap with GBP support documentation, crawl timing, insufficient internal-link equity, or Google preferring shorter AEO question pages for some queries.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/blog/google-business-profile-for-service-area-businesses

runs.bet: Hiring-comparison pages are more index-worthy when they make the scope tradeoff explicit: Glow Social is for baseline post production and review-ready consistency, while a manager is for strategy, engagement, campaigns, and judgment. The page should earn more qualified visibility by answering the replacement question without overclaiming.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for Glow Social vs social media manager, hiring a social media manager alternative, social media manager cost alternative, and AI vs social media manager queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume hiring-comparison intent is weak. Possible causes include duplicate question/blog routes, overlap with cost and AI-vs-manager pages, crawl timing, insufficient internal-link equity, or Google preferring a pricing/cost canonical for some queries.
runs.migration_target: https://boomp.net/blog/glow-social-vs-hiring-social-media-manager

runs.bet: Thin local pages are more index-worthy when they answer the local/industry question immediately, use safer review-ready product language, and route visitors to a concrete preview rather than a generic pricing CTA.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether these five local pages move from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions improve for industry + city social media management and affordable social media management local queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If these pages remain unindexed, do not assume all local pages are weak. Possible causes include local-page duplication, thin city/industry differentiation, crawl timing, weak internal links from hubs, or Google deciding only a smaller set of local pages should be indexed.
runs.migration_target: Boomp local-page strategy pending review before port

runs.bet: The proven local-page pattern should help another batch of thin local pages: answer the city/industry intent fast, clean up overclaims, and send visitors to a preview-first next step.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether these five local pages move from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions improve for industry + city social media management and affordable local-service social media queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If these pages remain unindexed, do not assume all local pages should be removed. Possible causes include local-page duplication, thin city/industry differentiation, crawl timing, weak hub links, or Google preferring fewer local variants.
runs.migration_target: Boomp local-page strategy pending review before port

runs.bet: Scheduler-benefits pages are more index-worthy when they answer both sides of the query: schedulers help with publishing consistency, but they do not solve the empty-calendar content burden. This page should support scheduler-related queries while routing owners toward preview-first content creation when scheduling is not the bottleneck.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for social media scheduler benefits, scheduler limits, social media scheduler for small business, and is a scheduler enough queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume scheduler intent is weak. Possible causes include the old generic slug, overlap with free/cheap scheduler pages, crawl timing, SERP competition, or insufficient internal-link equity.
runs.migration_target: Boomp port after branding is ready

runs.bet: This page is a strong fit for Glow's core buyer pain: owners do not want to become content creators, but they need the business to look current when customers check. A clearer snippet-style answer plus preview-first links should make the page more index-worthy and conversion-ready.
runs.expected_signal: Check whether the page moves from crawled/not indexed to indexed, and later whether impressions/clicks improve for keep business active without posting, social media without posting yourself, business looking active online, and no time to post social media queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If the page remains unindexed, do not assume the owner-burden intent is weak. Possible causes include crawl timing, overlap with no-time/minimum-viable/social-media-hate pages, the long slug, or insufficient internal-link equity.
runs.migration_target: Boomp port after branding is ready

runs.work_type: query-led title and snippet CTR refresh
runs.bet: The page is already earning broad visibility as a platform character-limit reference, not only a caption-length guide. If the title, description, and top answer explicitly match Instagram/TikTok/social media character limits and include captions, bios, notes, DMs, and official-help-center style phrasing, CTR should improve on high-impression page-one queries without sacrificing the existing caption-length footprint.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day page-specific query exports for higher CTR/clicks on current zero-click queries in positions 3-9, stable impressions for existing caption-length queries, and no major average-position loss on core Instagram/TikTok caption terms.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If CTR does not improve, possible causes include Google rewriting the title/snippet, official platform/help-center results satisfying the query directly, zero-click SERP behavior for numeric answers, or too short a measurement window after the title change.
runs.migration_target: Boomp port after branding is ready

runs.bet: The page is in the position 8-11 band with the largest impression opportunity. Broadening title/meta/top answer from freelance-only rates to social media manager cost across freelance, agency, hourly, and full-time options should improve position and CTR while preserving the existing freelance-rates footprint.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for movement from pos ~8.6 toward positions 5-7, CTR improvement from 0.15%, more clicks on social media manager cost/rates/freelance rate queries, and stable impressions for existing freelance-rate terms.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include Google rewriting the title/snippet, SERPs dominated by job-market or marketplace pages, page-specific query evidence needing a different title shape, or the page needing external authority beyond internal links.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: The page is the highest-impression zero-click comparison page in the position 8-11 set. A clearer Vista Social vs Glow Social 2026 title and top answer that spells out pricing, content creation, scheduling, analytics, and dashboard-vs-posts-ready decision logic should improve CTR and potentially average position for Vista Social comparison/alternative queries.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for clicks from the 0-click baseline, CTR improvement, movement from pos ~10.2 toward positions 6-8, and stable or improved impressions for Vista Social vs Glow Social, Vista Social alternative, Vista Social pricing/comparison, and scheduler vs done-for-you queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include Google rewriting the title/snippet, queries being mostly navigational for Vista Social, low Glow brand awareness in comparison SERPs, or the page needing more external authority/reviews rather than title and internal-link work.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: A thin but useful DIY-vs-agency calculator page should become more index-worthy and conversion-ready when it answers the hiring decision before the calculator, explains how to interpret the result, adds matching FAQ/Breadcrumb schema, and routes high-time-cost users toward a preview-first next step.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day indexing, impressions, clicks, CTR, average position, and assisted movement into /preview, /tools/social-media-management-cost-calculator, and cost/pricing pages for DIY vs agency social media cost and agency cost calculator queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include low exact-query volume, Google preferring the broader social media management cost calculator, insufficient internal-link equity, or needing page-specific query evidence before another title change. Do not assume tools/calculators are weak from one page.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: For simple numeric queries like Buffer free-plan limits, the title and first paragraph need to carry the answer directly. Reframing the page title around 3 channels and 10 queued posts, adding the queue-limit-not-monthly-limit answer earlier, and strengthening close scheduler links should improve CTR and position without competing with the full Buffer pricing guide.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for clicks from the 0-click baseline, CTR improvement, movement from pos ~9.1 toward positions 5-7, stable/improved impressions for Buffer free plan limits and Buffer 10 scheduled posts terms, and support lift into the full Buffer pricing guide.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include zero-click SERPs for simple numeric answers, Google preferring Buffer official docs, title/snippet rewriting, or the support page needing to consolidate more strongly into the full Buffer pricing guide.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: The page is a complete-guide hub in the position 8-11 band with zero clicks. Making the title, description, and top answer match best social media tools under $50/month in 2026 while preserving complete-guide coverage should improve CTR and average position for under-$50 social media tool searches and support adjacent Buffer/Later/Publer/Metricool/Plann comparison pages.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for clicks from the 0-click baseline, CTR improvement, movement from pos ~9.4 toward positions 5-7, stable/improved impressions for best social media tools under $50 and affordable/cheap social media tools, and support lift into adjacent scheduler/comparison pages.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include title/snippet rewriting, list SERPs preferring software marketplaces, weak external authority compared with tool-review sites, or the page needing a fuller source/comparison table rather than only title/top-answer work.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: Searchers looking for a Plann alternative are likely comparing visual planning tools, but the buyer-intent opportunity for Glow is owner burden: Plann organizes content that already exists; Glow Social prepares posts from the website for approval. Retitling around Best Plann Alternative 2026 and strengthening the quick answer around posts ready to approve should improve CTR and help the page compete for Plann alternative / visual planner alternative queries.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for CTR improvement from 0.16%, movement from pos ~9.7 toward positions 5-7, more clicks on Plann alternative / best Plann alternative / Plann vs Glow Social / visual planner alternative queries, support lift into Planoly and Canva Pro alternatives, and stable/improved impressions from the 639 baseline.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include low Plann-branded search volume, Google preferring software directories, title/snippet rewriting, or the page needing a fuller feature/pricing table instead of only title/top-answer/internal-link work.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: A thin ROI calculator page should become more index-worthy and conversion-ready when it answers the break-even-customer question before the tool, counts owner time as a real cost, adds FAQ/Breadcrumb schema, and routes realistic users toward a preview-first next step before they commit to a bigger retainer.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day indexing, impressions, clicks, CTR, average position, and assisted movement into /preview, /tools/social-media-management-cost-calculator, and pricing/cost pages for social media ROI calculator, social media ROI for local business, and social media break-even queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include low exact-query volume, ROI queries being satisfied by generic marketing calculators, insufficient internal-link equity, or needing page-specific query evidence before another title change. Do not assume tools/calculators are weak from one ROI page.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: A thin posting-frequency calculator page should become more index-worthy and conversion-ready when it answers the local-business cadence question before the tool, explains that owner time and content creation capacity constrain the schedule, adds FAQ/Breadcrumb schema, and routes high-burden users toward a preview-first next step.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day indexing, impressions, clicks, CTR, average position, and assisted movement into /preview, /tools, and posting-frequency benchmark pages for posting frequency calculator, how often should a local business post, and Google Business Profile posting cadence queries.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include low exact calculator-query volume, Google preferring broader benchmark guides, insufficient internal-link equity, or needing page-specific query evidence before another title change. Do not assume tools/calculators are weak from one cadence page.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move

runs.bet: The page had a useful URL and topic but thin coverage, stale pricing language, and overclaimy automation phrasing. Rebuilding it as an answer-first guide around best automated social media tools for small business in 2026, while clearly separating tools that schedule posts from tools that prepare posts, should improve CTR and make the page more credible for SEO/AEO retrieval.
runs.expected_signal: Check 7/14/28-day Pages and page-specific Queries exports for clicks from the 0-click baseline, CTR improvement, movement from pos ~10.8 toward positions 6-8, stable/improved impressions for automated social media tools and best social media automation tools small business, and support lift into the automation/tools-under-$50 clusters.
runs.do_not_overlearn_if: If weak, possible causes include low search volume, Google preferring software marketplaces, title/snippet rewriting, or the page needing a more robust externally sourced pricing/features table.
runs.migration_target: Re-sync latest Glow version before final Glow→Boomp URL move
